english: pa inhabitant of the earth, of our globe Sch. — sa-spyód (sa spyod) possessing the earth, man Cs. — sa-pág (sa phag) Glr., prob. = so-pág (so phag) brick, dried in the sun. — sa-púg (sa phug) cavern, cave. — sa-pyógs (sa phyogs) place, region, tract, ˳jigs-pai sa-pyógs ('jigs pa'i sa phyogs) an unsafe place or region Thgy. — *sá-bi-lig* (sa bi lig) W. 1. mouse, rat 2. Ld. also bar, bolt, door-bar? — sa-blá (sa bla) v. above sa (sa) 1. — sa-dbáṅ (sa dbang) Cs. = sa-bdág (sa bdag), v. above sa (sa) no. 1. — sa-˳bol (sa 'bol) Cs. soft earth. — sa-ma-rdó (sa ma rdo) or sa-min-rdó-min (sa min rdo min) Sch.: 'neither earth nor stone', i.e. a kind of conglomerate. — sa-min (sa min) Sch.: 'white sand' (??). — sa-mós (sa mos) Sch. = kù-mu-da (ku mu da) or úd-pa-la (ud pa la). — sa-dmár (sa dmar) red earth; sa-dmar-˳boṅ (sa dmar 'bong) n. of a monastery in the neighbourhood of Darjeeling, situated on a mountain-slope, which consists of a red-coloured soil (Hook. I, 171 calls it Simonbong). — *sa-tse* (sa tse) W. sand or gravel found in roasted barley etc. — sa-rtsig (sa rtsig), sa-rtsis (sa rtsis), sa-tsig (sa tshig) stage, post-station. — sa-mtsams (sa mtshams), W. *san-tsam* (san tsam), border, frontier, boundary Glr.; sa-mtsáms ˳gégs-pa (sa mtshams 'gegs pa) to fix the borders or limits, to mark out the boundaries. — sa-żag (sa zhag) dust floating on water Pth. — sa-gżi (sa gzhi) ground, soil, footing, floor; estate Tar. 99. — sa-˳óg (sa 'og) v. above sa (sa) 1, sa-˳og-spyód (sa 'og spyod) the Nagas (klu (klu)). — sa-yáṅs (sa yangs) a wide place or space, an extensive tract of land. — sa-yúl (sa yul) = sa-čá (sa cha) Glr. — sa-gyos (sa gyos), sa-gyó-ba (sa gyo ba) earthquake. — sa-rígs (sa rigs) species of earth. — sa-rís (sa ris) Pth.? — sa-rúl (sa rul) Cs. rotten or decayed earth (?) — *sa-ró* (sa ro) W. rubbish, (Sch. fallow-ground, fallow-field?) — sa-lám (sa lam) Mil., perh. for sa-bcui lam (sa bcu'i lam). — sa-śùn (sa shun) Sch. crust of the earth (?) — sa-gżin (sa gzhin) fertile land, rich soil. — sa-sróṅ (sa srong) hard ground. — sa-srúṅ (sa srung) Cs. = sa-bdag (sa bdag) v. above sa (sa) no. 1. — sa-srós (sa sros) evening twilight, dusk Cs.

a large monastery, S.W. of Lhasa, also the Lamas belonging to it, clothed in red, Wts. 132. Schl. 73. sa(-skya)-čéṅ(-po) (sa skya chen po) honorary title of the Lama Kun-dga-snyiṅ-po (kun dga' snying po), born in the year 1090 after Christ; sa-skyá paṇḍi-ta (sa skya paNDi ta) a famous Lama of this monastery, born 1180.
n. of one of the lunar mansions, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar) no. 15, and hence also n. of a month, part of March and April, ni f.
Tar. 184 and 187, Schf.: diploma, patent, not to be found in Ssk. dictionaries.

1. seed, sá-bon ˳débs-pa (sa bon 'debs pa) to sow, sá-bon btáb-mkan (sa bon btab mkhan) sower, sá-bon-du byéd-pa (sa bon du byed pa) to use as seed-corn Dzl.; seed-corn, corn, grain, also green corn, sá-bon tsám-la rṅá-ba (sa bon tsam la rnga ba) to mow off as if it were green corn Ma. — 2. = ku-krág (khu khrag), v. kù-ba (khu ba) Med.; also = kù-ba (khu ba), e.g. sá-bon ˳dzin-pa (sa bon 'dzin pa) conception S.g., sa-bon zág-pa (sa bon zag pa) emissio seminis Glr.; fig.: propagation, progeny, issue, sá-bon byed mi nus (sa bon byed mi nus) then no propagation can take place; sa-bon čig żog (sa bon cig zhog) 'propagate thyself!' — 3. fig.: dge-bai sá-bon (dge ba'i sa bon) the seeds or germs of virtue, sdig-pai sá-bon (sdig pa'i sa bon) the germs of vice, da sá-bon ma bskyéd-na (da sa bon ma bskyed na) if I do not now produce seeds viz. of virtue (else more frq.: 'a root of virtue') Dzl.; byaṅ-čúb-kyi sá-bon (byang chub kyi sa bon) Tar. — 4. W. soap, acc. to the Hind. صابن , more accurately صابون
a million; this number, however, is not much in use with Indians and Tibetans, whereas the lāk, ˳bum ('bum), 100,000, frq. serves to represent a very large sum.
(sovereign, 'father to a country') a not unfrequent perversion of the title sa-heb (sa heb) W.
also sar-sór (sar sor), coarse-grained and fine-grained (corn, seeds etc.) mixed together W.
n. of a lunar mansion, v. rgyu-skár (rgyu skar) 15.
Ssk. n. of an Indian tree, Shorea robusta, with which also some superstitious fables are connected.
Hind. सारङ्गी, a kind of violin,
Ssk. शालि, Oryza sativa, rice, as a plant; acc. to Sch. also Indian corn (?).
